zh.lifeSummary: 當Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz於1564年11月5日出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆時，他的父親Willim Jans35歲，母親Anna Thonis44歲。他和Mary Jans Lijsken至少有1個女兒。他去世於大約1630年在荷蘭福利曼德享年67歲。

nameMeanings.name: Janz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Armin, Franz, Guenter, Siegfried.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Jan   1. Compare   Johnson  .

Germanized form of Slovenian Janc: nickname from a vernacular spelling of janjec ‘lamb’, or a patronymic from (a short form of) the personal name Janez, an equivalent of John . Compare Jantz 2 and Yantz 2.

variants.name: Jan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammad, Mohammed, Ferdous, Momin, Sultan, Ahmad, Altaf, Ameen, Amin, Amina, Arif, Arshad.  French Pierre, Andre, Marcel, Pascale.

North German, Danish, Polish, Sorbian, and Croatian; Czech (also Ján); Slovak (Ján): from the personal name Jan (Slovak Ján), a shortened vernacular form of Latin Johannes (see John ). The Sorbian surname is found mainly in Germanized forms, Jahn and Jähn. See also Yan .
Slovenian: from the personal name Jan, a variant or a short form of Janez, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: Jantz
meanings: German: variant of   Janz   1, a patronymic from the personal name   Jan   1.

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Cornelis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an old Roman family name, Cornélius, which is of uncertain origin, possibly a derivative of Latin cornu ‘horn’. This was the name of a 3rd-century pope who is venerated as a saint. The name was particularly popular in the Low Countries, and immigrants contributed to its frequency in the north of England from the 1400s. However, it is now seldom used in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Rudolph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

historicEvents.description: William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.
The siege of Leiden between the rebels and the Spanish. 1574 The dikes were opened allowing a Dutch fleet to sail over the flood waters and rescue Leiden.

William of Orange (1533-1584), Prince of Orange (1538-1584), Count of Nassau (1559-1584), and first stadholder of the United Provinces of the Netherlands, was assassinated by Burgundian Balthasar Gerard (25) with a handgun. Philip II of Spain had called for a volunteer assassin due to William's reluctance take a public stand on religious issues. William was succeeded by his 17-year-old son, Maurice of Nassau.

historicEvents.title: The Golden Age
historicEvents.description: "The 17th century era, from roughly 1585 to 1672, was considered to be Amsterdam's ""Golden Age"". The city had formed a worldwide trading network with countries like Africa, Indonesia, Brazil, and North America. The city grew significantly during this time, and was regarded as the wealthiest city in the western world."
Britain waged wars against the Dutch.

historicEvents.title: Bank of Amsterdam opens
historicEvents.description: The Amsterdam Office (part of the Dutch East India Company) traded in it's own shares during 1602 and effectively formed the world's first stock exchange. In 1609, the Bank of Amsterdam began operations as a full service bank.

historicEvents.title: First Dutch Academy is Opened
historicEvents.description: "Samuel Coster, a Dutch playwright, helped establish the ""First Nederduytsche Academy"". It was created with the intent to offer higher education to common citizens and offer higher quality theatre than what was available before. The inauguration took place on September 23, 1617 and included two plays: ""Apollo"" by Suffridus Sixtinus and ""Vande moordt begaen aen Wilhelm van Orangien"" by Gijsbert van Hoghendorp."

en.lifeSummary: When Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz was born on 5 November 1564, in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Willim Jans, was 35 and his mother, Anna Thonis, was 44. He had at least 1 daughter with Mary Jans Lijsken. He died about 1630, in Friesland, Netherlands, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz est né le 5 novembre 1564, à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Willim Jans, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Anna Thonis, avait 44 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mary Jans Lijsken. Il est décédé environ 1630, à Frise, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz am 5. November 1564 in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Willim Jans 35 und seine Mutter Anna Thonis 44. Er hatte mit Mary Jans Lijsken mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1630 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Friesland, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz è nato il il 5 novembre 1564 nel Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Willim Jans, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Anna Thonis, aveva 44 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mary Jans Lijsken. Morì circa 1630, nel Friesland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 67 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz(이)가 1564년 11월 5일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Willim Jans(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Thonis(은)는 44세였다. 그는 Mary Jans Lijsken(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1630년 네덜란드 프리슬랜드에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz родился 5 ноября 1564 г. в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Willim Jans было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Thonis, 44. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mary Jans Lijsken. Он умер около 1630 г. в местности фриесланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Willem Cornelis Rudolph Janz nació el 5 de noviembre de 1564, en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Willim Jans, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Anna Thonis, tenía 44 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mary Jans Lijsken. Murió aproximadamente 1630, en Frisia, Países Bajos, a la edad de 67 años.
